title: 06033_The_Price_of_Differential_Privacy_under_Continual_Observation
positive_examples: The paper discusses the application of differential privacy in continual release models, which can enhance privacy in systems like COVID-19 dashboards and recommendation systems, thereby protecting individual data while providing useful statistics.
The mechanisms developed can improve the accuracy of data analysis in sensitive applications, such as public health and machine learning, while ensuring privacy.
negative_examples: The continual release model may lead to increased error rates in data outputs compared to batch models, which could mislead users relying on these statistics for decision-making.
The complexity of ensuring privacy in adaptive settings may lead to potential misuse or misunderstanding of the privacy guarantees, especially if users are not aware of the limitations.
